name: AI-PR-Code-Review
description: Automated Code Review for Pull Requests. Supports GitHub Copilot, OpenAI, Google Gemini, Claude, and Grok. Bring your own AI subscription to Azure DevOps.
author: Lawrence Shen

inputs.name: inputAiProvider
inputs.label: AI Provider
inputs.helpMarkDown: Select the AI provider to use for generating comments.

inputs.name: inputModelName
inputs.label: Gemini Model Name
inputs.helpMarkDown: Enter the Gemini model name. (e.g. gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-pro)

inputs.name: inputModelKey
inputs.label: Gemini API Key
inputs.helpMarkDown: Enter your API key for the selected AI provider.

inputs.name: inputOpenAIModelName
inputs.label: OpenAI Model Name
inputs.helpMarkDown: Enter the OpenAI model name (e.g. gpt-5, gpt-5-mini, gpt-5-nano, gpt-5-mini, gpt-5-mini-mini).

inputs.name: inputOpenAIApiKey
inputs.label: OpenAI API Key
inputs.helpMarkDown: Enter your OpenAI API key.

inputs.name: inputGrokModelName
inputs.label: Grok Model Name
inputs.helpMarkDown: Enter the Grok model name (e.g. grok-3-mini, grok-4-fast-non-reasoning).

inputs.name: inputGrokApiKey
inputs.label: Grok (xAI) API Key
inputs.helpMarkDown: Enter your Grok (xAI) API key.

inputs.name: inputClaudeModelName
inputs.label: Claude Model Name
inputs.helpMarkDown: Enter the Claude model name (e.g. claude-haiku-4-5).

inputs.name: inputClaudeApiKey
inputs.label: Claude API Key
inputs.helpMarkDown: Enter your Claude (Anthropic) API key.

inputs.name: inputGitHubCopilotToken
inputs.label: GitHub Copilot Token
inputs.helpMarkDown: GitHub Fine-grained Personal Access Token (github_pat_xxx) for authenticating with GitHub Copilot service. Required permissions: Account permissions > Copilot > Access: Read-only. Get from GitHub Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens > Fine-grained tokens. **Note: Classic tokens (ghp_) are not supported. Cannot be used together with CLI Server Address.**

inputs.name: inputGitHubCopilotModelName
inputs.label: GitHub Copilot Model Name
inputs.helpMarkDown: Optional. Defaults to gpt-5-mini.

inputs.name: inputGitHubCopilotServerAddress
inputs.label: GitHub Copilot CLI Server Address
inputs.helpMarkDown: (Optional) Enter GitHub Copilot CLI Server address (IP or Domain + Port). Example: 192.168.1.100:8080. If not provided, will use GitHub Copilot CLI in Build Agent. **Note: Cannot be used together with GitHub Token.**

inputs.name: inputGitHubCopilotCliPath
inputs.label: GitHub Copilot CLI Path
inputs.helpMarkDown: (Optional) Absolute path to Copilot CLI executable on the build agent. Example (Windows): C:\Tools\copilot\copilot.exe. If empty, task will fallback to environment variable COPILOT_CLI_PATH, then system PATH.

inputs.name: inputGitHubCopilotTimeout
inputs.label: GitHub Copilot Request Timeout (ms)
inputs.helpMarkDown: Request timeout in milliseconds for GitHub Copilot. Default: 120000 ms (2 minutes). If left empty, defaults to 60000 ms (1 minute).

inputs.name: inputSystemInstructionSource
inputs.label: System Instruction Source
inputs.helpMarkDown: Select the source of the system instruction.

inputs.name: inputSystemPromptFile
inputs.label: System Prompt File
inputs.helpMarkDown: Path to the system prompt file. Supported formats: .md, .txt, .json, .yaml, .yml, .xml, .html. Required if System Instruction Source is 'File'.

inputs.name: inputSystemInstruction
inputs.label: System Instruction
inputs.defaultValue:
You are a senior software engineer. Please help complete the PR code review and respond according to the following instructions.
1. Begin with a summary conclusion of the analysis, for example: AI Review Status: 🟢 Recommend Approval, 🔴 Recommend Rejection, 🟡 Needs Human Review, followed by a brief explanation within 100 characters, then use <hr/> for a line break.
2. Do not include any content unrelated to the code review.
3. Use Traditional Chinese (zh-TW) for the review result. Each issue should be listed as a bullet point. Use the following format: Emoji [Category] : Detailed explanation. Choose from: 🔴 [Critical], ⚠️ [Warning], 💡 [Suggestion], ✨ [Convention], or ❓ [Question].
4. Since each change may involve multiple modified files, mark each file before its corresponding review comments for easy reference.
5. If too many files are modified to analyze them all, limit the total response length to within 15,000 characters.
6. Skip analysis of images, binary files, or other non-code files.
7. Skip analysis of deleted files.
8. Use Markdown format for the reply.
9. Assume the provided code snippets are part of a larger, valid codebase. Do not report errors regarding "unresolved symbols," "missing definitions," or "reference issues" that may exist outside the provided diff. Focus your analysis strictly on the logic and quality of the changes themselves.
inputs.helpMarkDown: System-level instructions for the AI model to guide its behavior.

inputs.name: inputPromptTemplate
inputs.label: Prompt Template
inputs.helpMarkDown: Custom prompt template for the AI model. Use {code_changes} as a placeholder for the actual code changes.

inputs.name: inputMaxOutputTokens
inputs.label: Max Output Tokens
inputs.helpMarkDown: Maximum number of output tokens for the AI model's response.

inputs.name: inputTemperature
inputs.label: Temperature
inputs.helpMarkDown: Temperature setting for the AI model to control randomness in responses.

inputs.name: inputFileExtensions
inputs.label: File Extensions to Include
inputs.helpMarkDown: Comma-separated list of file extensions to include in the code review analysis. If left empty, all file types are included by default, except binary types.

inputs.name: inputBinaryExtensions
inputs.label: Binary File Extensions to Exclude
inputs.helpMarkDown: Comma-separated list of binary file extensions to exclude from the code review analysis. Default includes: .jpg, .jpeg, .png, .gif, .bmp, .ico, .webp, .pdf, .doc, .docx, .ppt, .pptx, .xls, .xlsx, .zip, .tar, .gz, .rar, .7z, .exe, .dll, .so, .dylib, .bin, .dat, .class, .mp3, .mp4, .avi, .mov, .flv

inputs.name: inputShowReviewContent
inputs.label: Show Review Content
inputs.helpMarkDown: When enabled, the code changes, system instruction, prompt, and AI response will be printed to the console for debugging purposes.

inputs.name: inputEnableThrottleMode
inputs.label: Enable AI Throttle Mode
inputs.helpMarkDown: When enabled (default), only code differences are sent to AI for review. When disabled, the entire new file content is sent to AI for review.

inputs.name: inputEnableIncrementalDiff
inputs.label: Enable Incremental Diff
inputs.helpMarkDown: When enabled, only the latest push changes are reviewed instead of all PR changes. Useful for repeated reviews on iteratively updated PRs. Expected savings: 30-90% of tokens depending on number of pushes.
